- Clicking a removed bar restores it (turns it gray)

- Rows 35-37: All removed (black)
- Row 39: All present (gray)
- Rows 41-43: All removed (black)
- Row 45: All removed (black)
- Rows 47-49: All removed (black)

The left side has row 33 removed, and all other bars present.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# First, remove row 33 col 1 (toggle it off)
result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 33, 'col': 39}])
print(f"Toggle row 33 col 1 off: level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

# Now restore rows 35-37, 41-43, 45, 47-49
# Horizontal bars at row 45: cols 38-40, 43-45, 48-50, 53-55
# Vertical bars at rows 35-37, 41-43, 47-49: cols 39, 44, 49, 54

actions = []
# Restore vertical bars in rows 35-37
for row in [36]:  # Middle of vertical bar group
    for col in [39, 44, 49, 54]:
        actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col})

# Restore horizontal bars in row 45
for col in [39, 44, 49, 54]:  # Centers of horizontal bars
    actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 45, 'col': col})

# Restore vertical bars in rows 41-43
for row in [42]:  # Middle of vertical bar group
    for col in [39, 44, 49, 54]:
        actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col})

# Restore vertical bars in rows 47-49
for row in [48]:  # Middle of vertical bar group
    for col in [39, 44, 49, 54]:
        actions.append({'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': row, 'col': col})

result = action(actions)
print(f"Restored bars: level_completed={result.get('level_completed')}")

# Check what changed
curr_ascii = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nCurrent right side bar area (rows 33-49, cols 33-56):")
for r in range(33, 50):
    print(f"  {r}: {curr_ascii[r][33:57]}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
